evals.prompt: We need a short PRD for changing project-advisor's planning policy: generated planning artifacts should go under action-items, but existing files must never be overwritten without an explicit replacement action. This is a small maintainer-facing clarification, not a new UI. Inspect the repository for current behavior and terminology. I have not decided whether replacement should be an error, a confirmation, or a versioned copy, so do not silently choose one. Produce the reviewable PRD, but do not split it into issues yet.
evals.expected_output: A repo-grounded document-focused PRD bundle that preserves the unresolved replacement policy as an open question or clearly labeled assumption, selects only useful blocks, and stops pending human acceptance.
expectations: The run inspects repository evidence and distinguishes confirmed behavior from the unresolved replacement policy.
The run authors a schema-version-1 YAML manifest rather than receiving a completed manifest as input.
The generated bundle contains index.html, prd.yaml, and local assets under action-items/PRD-<slug>/.
The PRD stays document-focused and does not invent specialized UI, workflow, API, data, or architecture surfaces.
The run performs structural review, identifies any visual checks it could not perform, and does not invoke issue splitting before acceptance.

evals.prompt: Draft a PRD for a maintainer review queue for generated PRDs. Reviewers need to distinguish empty, ready, blocked, and accepted states and understand why a draft is blocked. The queue should be read-only for the first release; editing remains in the existing authoring workflow. Inspect the repo before deciding terminology or where this belongs. I am unsure whether accepted PRDs should remain visible by default, so keep that decision open unless repository evidence settles it. Generate the standard review bundle and stop for my review.
evals.expected_output: A validated UI-focused PRD bundle that uses relevant wireframes, annotated states, or a read-only prototype to clarify hierarchy and behavior while keeping acceptance visibility unresolved.
expectations: The PRD uses repo-grounded terminology and records the accepted-item visibility decision as open or provisional.
The authored manifest selects ui-heavy with document and ui review surfaces for a product reason.
Visual blocks clarify empty, ready, blocked, and accepted states and explicitly remain read-only.
Requirements use stable IDs and connect to validation outcomes or explicit exceptions.

The run completes structural and traceability review, defers rendered responsive, print, and accessibility judgment to human review when no cheap preview path exists, and requests acceptance before issue splitting.

evals.prompt: Create a PRD for the lifecycle of a generated PRD: draft, human review, requested revision, acceptance, and an optional handoff to issue splitting. A reviewer must be able to reject a draft back to revision, and issue creation must never happen before acceptance. Inspect the repository for the current to-prd and to-issues contracts. We have not decided whether acceptance can later be revoked or who is authorized to accept, so surface those decisions instead of inventing workflow rules. Produce the PRD bundle and wait for review.
evals.expected_output: A workflow-focused PRD bundle that makes transitions, acceptance boundaries, failure paths, and unresolved authorization or revocation policy reviewable.
expectations: The run inspects both PRD authoring and issue-handoff repository contracts.
The authored manifest selects workflow-heavy with document and workflow review surfaces.
The selected blocks expose the draft, review, revision, acceptance, and optional handoff sequence plus failure paths.
Acceptance revocation and authorization remain explicit open questions unless supported by repository evidence.
The run does not create issues and requests human acceptance only after generation and review.

evals.prompt: Write a PRD for a stable readiness-evaluation API used by internal planning tools. Consumers submit a PRD manifest and receive an overall readiness result, blocking findings, advisory findings, and a recommended next action. Malformed input and unsupported schema versions need observable error behavior. Inspect the repository for the manifest and validation vocabulary. Do not choose transport, authentication, persistence, or deployment architecture unless the repo already constrains them. Generate the review bundle and stop before issue splitting.
evals.expected_output: An API-focused PRD bundle defining observable request, response, validation, and failure behavior while leaving unsupported implementation choices open.
expectations: The API vocabulary and constraints are grounded in the repository's manifest and validation contracts.
The authored manifest selects api-heavy with document and api review surfaces.
The API contract separates blocking findings, advisories, overall readiness, next action, malformed input, and unsupported-version behavior.
Transport, authentication, persistence, and deployment remain open unless repository evidence supports a decision.
Requirements have stable IDs and validation relationships or explicit exceptions.

evals.prompt: Create a PRD for preserving traceability when a PRD is regenerated from an edited manifest. Requirements, decisions, risks, questions, and testing outcomes should keep durable identities when their meaning has not changed, and broken relationships must be reported before publication. Inspect the current generator and validation code. We have not decided how to handle a renamed entity whose intent is unchanged or whether deleted IDs can ever be reused. Keep those policies explicit and unresolved if the repo does not answer them. Generate the PRD bundle for review only.
evals.expected_output: A data-focused PRD bundle explaining durable entities, relationships, lifecycle rules, validation, and unresolved rename or ID-reuse policy without overcommitting storage details.
expectations: The PRD is grounded in current traceability normalization and publication validation behavior.
The authored manifest selects data-heavy with document and data review surfaces.
Stable entities, relationships, regeneration, and broken-reference behavior are explicit and testable.
Rename handling and deleted-ID reuse remain open questions unless repository evidence resolves them.
Any data visual has a useful description or structured text equivalent.

evals.prompt: Draft a PRD for separating product judgment from deterministic PRD generation in project-advisor. The agent should own interviewing, repository grounding, uncertainty, and block selection; a standard-library generator should own schema validation, escaping, canonical rendering, asset copying, structural validation, and atomic publication. Inspect the current implementation and identify the real boundary. The future packaging and version-migration strategy is not settled, so do not fabricate it. Produce an architecture-oriented review bundle and stop before issue splitting.
evals.expected_output: An architecture-focused PRD bundle that accurately describes agent and generator boundaries, portability, failure handling, repository evidence, and open evolution questions.
expectations: The PRD accurately separates current agent responsibilities from deterministic generator responsibilities using repository evidence.
The authored manifest selects architecture-heavy with document and architecture review surfaces.
Architecture visuals include descriptions or structured text and clarify actual boundaries rather than decorative components.
Failure handling and atomic publication behavior are represented without turning evidence paths into mandatory implementation instructions.
Packaging and schema-migration strategy remain open unless the repository already settles them.
